title: Nail Polish on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 30 minutes to several hours; fiber + code checks before solvent
stainChemistry: Same chemistry as nail polish on carpet: nitrocellulose (film former) + plasticizers + concentrated pigment + volatile solvents (ethyl acetate, butyl acetate, sometimes toluene). Solvents flash off fast; nitrocellulose hardens into a tough pigmented film that mechanically locks into fiber. Upholstery adds TWO risks not present on carpet: (1) upholstery CODE — many upholstery fabrics are S-only (solvent cleaning only) or have delicate backing that can't tolerate water rinses, and (2) CUSHION BATTING — if polish soaks through into foam, home chemistry can't reach it. Also: acetone DESTROYS acetate, modacrylic, and triacetate fibers on contact — upholstery includes lots of these in microfiber and suede-like fabrics. Fiber check + code check are both non-negotiable.
whyThisWorks: Acetone-free nail polish remover softens nitrocellulose safely on most fibers. Tamping with catch cloth pattern (paper towels under, solvent cloth on top tamping) pulls dissolved polish down and out. Dawn emulsifies solvent and residue on W/W-S code. For S-code, dry buff the solvent rather than adding water. The limitation: fully dried polish in a tight weave, or polish that soaked into cushion batting, is often a pro-only job.
defaultAssumption: Home manicure accident on couch / chair / bed. Fresh, caught in the first few minutes before full polymerization = real chance. Dried with film set, or spill went into cushion = pro or accept. Microfiber and suede-like fabrics are often acetone-sensitive — acetone-free only. If cushion cover zips off and is washable, freeze the spot first and wash the cover cold.

spottingProtocol.agent: Code + Fiber Check — Before Any Solvent
spottingProtocol.equipment: Good light, acetone-free remover, white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Upholstery code: W / W-S = full protocol. S-only = solvent only (no Dawn-in-water rinse). X = vacuum only, pro. Fiber test: dab ACETONE-FREE nail polish remover on a white cloth, press on a hidden upholstery seam 30 sec. Color transfer / fiber change / texture shift = STOP, pro. Clean = proceed with acetone-free remover. NEVER start with acetone on unknown fabric — acetate / modacrylic / triacetate + acetone = destroyed fiber.

spottingProtocol.agent: Blot Wet Polish or Scrape Dried — Straight Down
spottingProtocol.instruction: Wet polish: press paper towels STRAIGHT DOWN. Lift straight up, repeat with fresh ones until no more transfers. Dried polish: scrape the surface film off with the edge of a plastic card, working outward from center. Don't dig.
Dust cornstarch over the spot for 10 min. Absorbs remaining liquid polish. Vacuum off.

spottingProtocol.agent: Catch Cloth UNDER + Acetone-Free Solvent on Top
spottingProtocol.equipment: White catch cloth under, acetone-free remover on separate white cloth on top
spottingProtocol.instruction: Place a catch cloth UNDER (where possible — zip-off cushion cover makes this easy). Apply acetone-free remover to a white cloth, tamp from the top, outside-in. Flip cloth as it picks up pigment. Never pour solvent on upholstery. 3–5 passes typical for fresh. Ventilate — flammable.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dawn Damp Cloth (W / W-S Code Only)
spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: W / W-S: Dawn in cool water on damp cloth, work with soft toothbrush to lift residual pigment, blot. S-only: dry-buff solvent residue instead, no water added. Damp cloth only, never pour.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dry towel + weight, fan, open window
spottingProtocol.instruction: Weighted dry towel 30 min. Fan with windows open several hours to vent solvent vapor. Inspect in daylight. Residual pigment = repeat steps 4–5.

steps: 1. Code tag + fiber hidden-seam test with acetone-free.
2. Blot wet / scrape dried polish — no wiping.
4. Catch cloth under + acetone-free tamp from top, outside-in.
5. Dawn damp cloth (W/W-S) or dry buff (S).
7. Plateau = pro amyl acetate / reduce pigment.
products: Acetone-free nail polish remover (ethyl acetate or propylene carbonate based)
professionalProtocol.temperature: ambient — no heat until solvent fully evaporated
warnings: FIBER CHECK FIRST — acetone destroys acetate / modacrylic / triacetate
Never pour solvent on upholstery — tamp with cloth
Cushion batting saturation = pro — home can't reach foam

homeSolutions.instruction: Before any remover touches the upholstery, two things: (1) FABRIC TEST — is it safe? Acetone DESTROYS acetate, modacrylic, and triacetate on contact, and those fibers are common in microfiber and suede-like fabrics. Dab acetone-FREE nail polish remover on a hidden seam first. If it picks up color / fiber / texture, stop and call a pro. (2) CODE TAG — find the tag under the cushion. W / W-S = full protocol works. S-only = solvent only, no Dawn + water rinse. X = vacuum only, pro. If the cushion cover zips off and is machine-washable, the best path is freeze + scrape + wash the cover cold.

homeSolutions.agent: Blot Wet Polish Straight Down — or Scrape Dried
homeSolutions.instruction: WET polish: press paper towels STRAIGHT DOWN on the spot. Lift straight up, press fresh ones, repeat until no more color transfers. Do NOT wipe — wiping spreads pigment across clean fabric. DRIED polish: scrape the surface film off with the edge of a plastic card, working outward from the center. Don't dig into the fabric.
Dust cornstarch over the remaining pigment. Wait 10 minutes. Absorbs lingering wet polish and prevents it from spreading when you apply solvent. Vacuum off with an upholstery attachment.

homeSolutions.agent: Acetone-Free Remover on a White Cloth — Tamp Outside-In
homeSolutions.instruction: Apply ACETONE-FREE nail polish remover (the gentler kind — ethyl acetate or propylene carbonate based) to a clean white cloth. NEVER pour solvent on upholstery — pouring sends it into cushion batting where you can't reach it. Press and tamp the spot from the TOP. Flip the cloth as it picks up pigment. Work from the OUTSIDE of the stain toward the CENTER — counter-intuitive but prevents spreading. If possible (zip-off cushion cover, loose cushion), slip a white catch cloth UNDER the fabric so pigment transfers DOWN into it. 3–5 passes for fresh polish. Open windows — flammable.

If acetone-free remover runs out of effect, regular acetone is stronger BUT only safe if your hidden-seam test was COMPLETELY clean and you're confident the fiber isn't acetate / modacrylic / triacetate. Re-test acetone at the hidden seam first. Any doubt = stop and go pro. Pure acetone on upholstery is a high-stakes move.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn Damp Cloth OR Dry Buff — Code-Dependent
homeSolutions.instruction: W / W-S code: work a drop of Dawn in cool water into a damp cloth, press on the spot 3–5 minutes, blot clean. S-only code: DO NOT add water. Dry-buff the solvent residue with a clean dry cloth instead. Damp cloth only in either case — no pouring.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel + Fan, Windows Open
homeSolutions.instruction: Stack a dry towel on the spot, weigh with a book, 30 min. Point a fan at the area for several hours with windows still open to vent solvent vapor. Don't close the room up while solvent is still evaporating. Inspect in daylight after full dry. Residual pigment = repeat steps 4–6.

homeSolutions.agent: Escalate for S-Only, Wool, Silk, Microfiber Issues, or Deep Soak
homeSolutions.instruction: Call a pro if: the code is S-only and acetone-free plateaued, the upholstery is wool / silk, the hidden-seam test showed fiber interaction, polish soaked into cushion batting, or microfiber shows texture change after solvent. Tell them: "Nail polish on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, fabric [microfiber / velvet / cotton / silk / unknown], age [fresh / dried], home-treated with cornstarch + acetone-free remover tamped from top + [Dawn / dry buff]. Fiber test was [clean / showed pickup]. [Residual pigment / batting saturation / fiber concern]."

steps: 1. FIBER CHECK + CODE CHECK — both non-negotiable.
2. Blot wet straight down or scrape dried film.
4. Catch cloth under + acetone-free remover on cloth, tamp from top, outside-in. Never pour.
5. Graduate to acetone ONLY if fiber-tested clean — otherwise stop.
6. Dawn damp cloth (W/W-S) OR dry buff (S-only).
7. Weighted dry towel + fan-dry with ventilation.
8. S-only plateau / wool / silk / batting soak = pro.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if the hidden-seam test damages upholstery, if code is S-only and acetone-free plateaus, if the cushion batting is saturated, or if the upholstery is wool / silk / leather.
warnings: FIBER CHECK FIRST — acetone destroys several common upholstery fibers
Flammable solvent — ventilate, no flame, no smoking
Never wipe polish — blot straight down or tamp outside-in

neverDo: Never wipe — blot straight down or tamp
fiberSensitivities: W / W-S upholstery: tolerates acetone-free + Dawn + cornstarch
S-only: acetone-free remover, dry-buff — no water rinse
Microfiber: often acetate blend — acetone-free ONLY, test carefully
Acetate / modacrylic / triacetate: acetone DESTROYS — pro

materialWarnings: Microfiber + acetone is a high-risk combo

escalation.when: Hidden-seam test damages fiber, code is S-only with plateau, cushion batting shows polish soak-through, upholstery is wool / silk / microfiber with solvent issues, or acetone-free can't finish and acetone isn't fiber-safe.
escalation.whatToTell: "Nail polish on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, fabric [microfiber / velvet / cotton / silk / unknown], age [fresh / dried], home-treated with cornstarch + acetone-free remover tamped from top + [Dawn / dry buff]. Fiber test [clean / showed pickup]. [Residual pigment / batting soak / fiber concern]."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with solvent spotting + fiber ID

contextQuestions: Upholstery code — W / W-S / S / X?
Fabric — microfiber / velvet / woven cotton / silk / leather?
Does the cushion cover zip off and machine-wash?
Dark color (red / black / purple) or light (pastel / nude)?
Has anyone already tried acetone on it?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Microfiber is the subtle trap — often an acetate/polyester blend, and acetone softens the acetate. Always acetone-free on microfiber. Zip-off washable cushion cover is the easiest path when available. Dark pigments (red, black, purple) often leave a ghost even after the film is gone — pigment is separate chemistry.
